DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TOWNSHIP BUILDING SERVICES, INC., WESTFIELD SF CENTER LP, WESTFIELD PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP NAMED AS WESTFIELD DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants Township Building Services, Inc., Westfield SF Center LP, Westfield Property Management and SF Center Limited Partnership's demurrer is OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART.

See WORD MINUTES.

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANTS TOWNSHIP BUILDING SERVICES, INC., WESTFIELD SF CENTER LP, WESTFIELD PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, and S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP's, NAMED AS WESTFIELD, MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Township Building Services, Inc., Westfield SF Center LP, Westfield Property Management and SF Center Limited Partnership's motion to strike is DENIED.

"Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof," (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (Code of Civil Procedure section 436.) "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (Code of Civil Procedure section 452.)

Here, Defendants' motion fails because it is based on a condition precedent that has not come to pass. Defendants contend Plaintiff's punitive damages allegations and prayer should be stricken because they are premised on Plaintiff's tort claims-the first and second causes of action, which cannot survive demurrer. Those claims, however, survived demurrer. Thus the motion to strike must be denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
